title: Subject pronouns and greetings
summary: Core subject pronouns and everyday greetings for basic introductions.

items.french: Je / Tu / Il / Elle / On / Nous / Vous / Ils / Elles
items.english: I / You informal / He / She / We-one / We / You formal-plural / They masculine-mixed / They feminine
items.note: Start by recognizing the subject before conjugating the verb.

items.french: Je suis étudiant. / Tu habites à Vancouver. / Nous parlons français.
items.english: I am a student. / You live in Vancouver. / We speak French.
items.note: Short subject-pronoun examples with common A1 verbs.

items.french: Bonjour / Bon après-midi / Bonsoir / Salut / Bonne nuit / À demain / Merci / S'il vous plaît / Au revoir
items.english: Hello-good morning / Good afternoon / Good evening / Hi / Good night / See you tomorrow / Thank you / Please / Goodbye
items.note: These are the most common polite phrases for basic conversation.

title: Articles and gender
summary: Definite and indefinite articles plus common noun gender patterns.

items.french: le / la / l' / les
items.english: the masculine / the feminine / the before vowel / the plural
items.note: Use `l'` before a vowel or silent h.

items.french: le livre / la voiture / l'école / les étudiants
items.english: the book / the car / the school / the students
items.note: Basic examples of the four definite article forms.

items.french: un / une / des
items.english: a masculine / a feminine / some-plural
items.note: Use these for non-specific nouns.

items.french: un livre / une maison / des amis
items.english: a book / a house / some friends
items.note: Simple examples to memorize with article + noun together.

items.french: Usually masculine: -age / -ment / -eau / -isme
items.english: Often masculine endings: -age / -ment / -eau / -isme
items.note: Examples: `le voyage`, `le document`, `le bateau`, `le tourisme`.

items.french: Usually feminine: -tion / -té / -esse / -ie / -ure
items.english: Often feminine endings: -tion / -té / -esse / -ie / -ure
items.note: Examples: `la nation`, `la liberté`, `la vitesse`, `la pharmacie`, `la voiture`. These are patterns, not guarantees.

title: Être and avoir
summary: The two most important verbs at A1, with core examples.

items.french: je suis / tu es / il-elle-on est / nous sommes / vous êtes / ils-elles sont
items.english: I am / you are / he-she-one is / we are / you are / they are
items.note: Memorize `être` early because it appears everywhere.

items.french: Je suis étudiant. / Elle est canadienne. / Nous sommes fatigués.
items.english: I am a student. / She is Canadian. / We are tired.
items.note: Simple identity and description examples.

items.french: j'ai / tu as / il-elle-on a / nous avons / vous avez / ils-elles ont
items.english: I have / you have / he-she-one has / we have / you have / they have
items.note: Another core irregular verb used constantly in French.

items.french: J'ai vingt ans. / Nous avons une voiture. / Ils ont un chien.
items.english: I am twenty years old. / We have a car. / They have a dog.
items.note: French uses `avoir` for age: `J'ai 20 ans.` literally `I have 20 years`.

title: Present tense basics
summary: Regular present-tense patterns plus two high-frequency irregular verbs.

items.french: parler: parle / parles / parle / parlons / parlez / parlent
items.english: to speak: speak / speak / speaks / speak / speak / speak
items.note: Pattern: `e`, `es`, `e`, `ons`, `ez`, `ent`.

items.french: finir: finis / finis / finit / finissons / finissez / finissent
items.english: to finish: finish / finish / finishes / finish / finish / finish
items.note: Pattern: `is`, `is`, `it`, `issons`, `issez`, `issent`.

items.french: faire: fais / fais / fait / faisons / faites / font
items.english: to do-make: do / do / does / do / do / do
items.note: Example: `Je fais mes devoirs.` / `Vous faites quoi ?`

items.french: aller: vais / vas / va / allons / allez / vont
items.english: to go: go / go / goes / go / go / go
items.note: Examples: `Je vais au cinéma.` / `Nous allons à l'école.`

title: Questions and il y a
summary: Three ways to ask questions, common question words, and how to say there is-there are.

items.french: Tu parles français ? / Est-ce que tu parles français ? / Parles-tu français ?
items.english: You speak French? / Do you speak French? / Do you speak French?
items.note: Main A1 question methods: intonation, `est-ce que`, and inversion.

items.french: Qui / Que-Qu' / Quel-Quelle-Quels-Quelles / Où / Quand / Pourquoi / Comment / Combien
items.english: Who / What / Which-what / Where / When / Why / How / How many
items.note: `Quel` agrees with the noun: `quel`, `quelle`, `quels`, `quelles`.

items.french: Qui est-ce ? / Que fais-tu ? / Quel âge as-tu ?
items.english: Who is it? / What are you doing? / How old are you?
items.note: These show `qui`, `que`, and `quel` in short everyday questions.

items.french: Où habites-tu ? / Quand partons-nous ? / Pourquoi apprends-tu le français ?
items.english: Where do you live? / When are we leaving? / Why are you learning French?
items.note: Useful model questions with common verbs.

items.french: Comment ça va ? / Combien d'étudiants y a-t-il ? / Quelle heure est-il ?
items.english: How are you? / How many students are there? / What time is it?
items.note: These cover `comment`, `combien`, and `quelle`.

items.french: Il y a un livre. / Il y a des étudiants. / Il n'y a pas de café.
items.english: There is a book. / There are students. / There is no coffee.
items.note: Use `il y a` for there is-there are. In the negative, use `de`.

items.french: Y a-t-il un restaurant ? / Est-ce qu'il y a un bus ?
items.english: Is there a restaurant? / Is there a bus?
items.note: Two common ways to ask whether something exists.

title: Place, time, and weather
summary: Prepositions of place, telling time and the date, and basic weather vocabulary.

items.french: à / dans / sur / sous / devant / derrière / entre / à côté de / en face de / près de / loin de
items.english: at-in / in / on / under / in front of / behind / between / next to / opposite / near / far from
items.note: Example: `Le livre est sur la table.`

items.french: Le chat est sous la chaise. / L'école est près de la maison. / Le café est en face de la gare.
items.english: The cat is under the chair. / The school is near the house. / The cafe is opposite the station.
items.note: These help fix `sous`, `près de`, and `en face de` in context.

items.french: La voiture est devant l'hôtel. / Le sac est à côté de la porte. / Le parc est entre la banque et la poste.
items.english: The car is in front of the hotel. / The bag is next to the door. / The park is between the bank and the post office.
items.note: Useful everyday location sentences.

items.french: Quelle heure est-il ? / Il est deux heures. / Il est deux heures et quart. / Il est deux heures et demie. / Il est trois heures moins le quart. / Il est midi. / Il est minuit.
items.english: What time is it? / It is two o'clock. / It is quarter past two. / It is half past two. / It is quarter to three. / It is noon. / It is midnight.
items.note: `et quart` = :15, `et demie` = :30, `moins le quart` = :45.

items.french: Quelle est la date aujourd'hui ? / Quel jour sommes-nous ? / Nous sommes le lundi 7 avril 2025.
items.english: What is today's date? / What day is it? / It is Monday, April 7, 2025.
items.note: Useful date formulas for everyday conversation.

items.french: Il fait beau / Il fait chaud / Il fait froid / Il pleut / Il neige / Il y a du soleil / Il y a du vent
items.english: Nice weather / It is hot / It is cold / It is raining / It is snowing / It is sunny / It is windy
items.note: Basic weather expressions to recognize and use.

items.french: le printemps / l'été / l'automne / l'hiver
items.english: spring / summer / autumn / winter
items.note: Core seasonal vocabulary.

title: Basic vocabulary
summary: Useful connectors, family words, colors, and numbers for simple A1 sentences.

items.french: et / mais / ou / donc / parce que / puis / aussi / d'abord / ensuite / enfin
items.english: and / but / or / therefore / because / then / also / first / then / finally
items.note: Example: `Je suis fatigué parce que je travaille beaucoup.`

items.french: le père / la mère / le frère / la sœur / le fils / la fille / l'oncle / la tante / le cousin / la cousine / les grands-parents
items.english: father / mother / brother / sister / son / daughter / uncle / aunt / male cousin / female cousin / grandparents
items.note: High-frequency family vocabulary.

items.french: blanc-blanche / noir-noire / vert-verte / bleu-bleue / gris-grise / rouge / jaune / orange / rose
items.english: white / black / green / blue / grey / red / yellow / orange / pink
items.note: Some color adjectives change for gender and some stay the same.

items.french: zéro / un / deux / trois / quatre / cinq / six / sept / huit / neuf / dix / onze / douze / treize / quatorze / quinze / seize / dix-sept / dix-huit / dix-neuf / vingt
items.english: zero / one / two / three / four / five / six / seven / eight / nine / ten / eleven / twelve / thirteen / fourteen / fifteen / sixteen / seventeen / eighteen / nineteen / twenty
items.note: Memorize these as a base before moving on to larger numbers.

title: Essential exam sentences
summary: Simple ready-made sentences for introductions, likes, family, and daily life.

items.french: Je m'appelle Sparsh. / J'ai 22 ans. / Je suis étudiant. / J'habite à Vancouver. / Je parle anglais et un peu français.
items.english: My name is Sparsh. / I am 22 years old. / I am a student. / I live in Vancouver. / I speak English and a little French.
items.note: Basic self-introduction model.

items.french: J'aime le sport. / J'aime la musique. / J'aime voyager.
items.english: I like sports. / I like music. / I like traveling.
items.note: Easy opinion sentences for beginner speaking.

items.french: J'ai une sœur. / J'ai deux parents. / Ma famille est importante.
items.english: I have a sister. / I have two parents. / My family is important.
items.note: Short family-description sentences.

items.french: Je travaille. / J'étudie à l'université. / Je vais à l'école.
items.english: I work. / I study at university. / I go to school.
items.note: Basic present-tense sentences for routine and daily life.
